---
title: Basic Usage
description: set, get, wrap, delete, batch operations, and namespaces.
icon: Play
---

## set and get

Store a value with an optional TTL and retrieve it:

```ts
import { cache } from "@/lib/cache";

// Set with TTL
await cache.set("user:1", { name: "Alice" }, { ttl: "5m" });

// Get
const user = await cache.get("user:1");
// → { name: "Alice" } or null if expired/missing
```

## wrap (cache-aside)

`wrap` is the primary pattern for caching: it returns the cached value on a hit, or calls the provided function on a miss, caches the result, and returns it.

```ts
const user = await cache.wrap(
  "user:1",
  () => db.users.findById(1),
  { ttl: "10m", tags: ["user:1"] }
);
```

## delete and has

```ts
await cache.delete("user:1");

const exists = await cache.has("user:1");
```

## pull (get-and-delete)

Retrieve a value and remove it atomically — useful for one-time tokens:

```ts
const token = await cache.pull("reset-token:abc123");
```

## setNx (set if not exists)

```ts
const set = await cache.setNx("lock:resource", "owner-id", { ttl: "30s" });
// → true if set, false if key already existed
```

## Batch operations

```ts
// Get multiple keys at once
const values = await cache.getMany(["user:1", "user:2", "user:3"]);

// Set multiple entries
await cache.setMany([
  { key: "a", value: 1, options: { ttl: "1m" } },
  { key: "b", value: 2, options: { ttl: "1m" } },
]);

// Delete multiple keys
await cache.deleteMany(["a", "b"]);

// wrap for multiple keys (fetches only misses)
const results = await cache.wrapMany(
  ["user:1", "user:2"],
  async (missingKeys) => {
    const users = await db.users.findMany(missingKeys.map((k) => k.split(":")[1]));
    return users.map((u) => ({ key: `user:${u.id}`, value: u }));
  },
  { ttl: "5m" }
);
```

## Namespace

Create an isolated sub-cache with a key prefix. All operations are scoped to that prefix:

```ts
const userCache = cache.namespace("user");

await userCache.set("1", data);       // stored as "user:1"
const value = await userCache.get("1");
```

## Tags

Tag entries to invalidate them as a group:

```ts
await cache.set("post:1", post, { tags: ["post:1", "user:1:posts"] });
await cache.set("post:2", post2, { tags: ["post:2", "user:1:posts"] });

// Invalidate all posts for user 1
await cache.invalidateTag("user:1:posts");
```

See [Tags](/docs/cache/tags) for details.

## Default TTL

Configure a default TTL when creating the cache:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createMemoryAdapter(),
  defaults: { ttl: "1h" },
});
```

## Error handling

Control behavior when an adapter operation fails:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createRedisAdapter({ client: redis }),
  onError: "silent", // "throw" | "silent" | "fallback"
});
```

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|---------|
| `"throw"` | Re-throw the adapter error (default) |
| `"silent"` | Swallow the error, return `null` |
| `"fallback"` | Return `null` on read errors, skip on write errors |
